reason: Defining integral has support [0, x] with kernel exp(-y t^2). C-198 Phase 4 verified that varying y rescales WITHIN the erf-tower but does not switch towers.

Same support [0, x] as T_erf but kernel sin(y t)/t is structurally distinct: trigonometric vs Gaussian. C-198 Phase 2 stage B (with Si in the alphabet) found ZERO non-trivial elementary combinations yielding T_erf or vice versa.

Defining integral has support [0, π], structurally distinct from any [0, x]-bounded integral. C-198 Phase 4 kernel cos(y sin(t)) gives partial-Bessel but NOT the full family without the explicit [0, π] support.
Defining integral has support [0, ∞) with cubic-phase oscillator kernel cos(t³/3 + x t). C-198 Phase 4 kernel 4 confirmed the cubic phase is unique to Airy and unreachable from Bessel/erf integrals.

Defining integral has support [0, ∞) with kernel t^(x-1) exp(-y t). At integer/half-integer y this reduces to elementary or erf, but Gamma at general y is a 2-parameter Pfaffian function distinct from the erf tower.
Defined as inverse of x*exp(x). NOT an integral operator at all — implicit/inversion type. C-198 Phase 3 verified W cannot be reached from {EML, erf, Si, J, Ai, Gamma} at any finite depth.

Defining integral has support [0, π/2] with kernel 1/sqrt(1 - m sin²(t)). The elliptic-modular-form structure is non-Pfaffian for full elliptic functions but the K, E, F, Pi-integrals are Pfaffian and structurally distinct from Bessel.

SERIES generator (not integral). Many other towers are SPECIAL CASES of hypergeometric functions at specific parameters: erf as ₁F₁, Bessel as ₀F₁, Legendre/Jacobi as ₂F₁. But the GENERAL hypergeometric is not derivable from any single integral kernel at finite depth — different generator type.

Lerch transcendent Phi(z, s, a) = Sum z^n / (n+a)^s generalizes polylog (a=1), Hurwitz zeta (z=1), Dirichlet eta (parametric specialization), and Lerch itself. eml_cost.analyze reports pfaffian_r=4 for zeta, Hurwitz zeta, Lerch, Dirichlet eta; pfaffian_r=3 for polylog (subsumed at chain 3). Mirrors the T_F supertower pattern.
promotion_criteria: Add 5+ Lerch-family rows to a C-207-like dataset; verify pfaffian_r matches chain_order=4 on all rows; document literature non-reduction to T_F or T_Gamma.
promotion_evidence: exploration/9th-tower-promotion-2026-04-27/: 25/25 hits (11/11 single-primitive + 14/14 composite) under chain-order additivity rule; 10/10 addendum rows in dataset_addendum.csv. Combined PNE corpus now 28/28 exact under the rule.

reason: Solutions of Mathieu's ODE y'' + (a - 2q*cos(2x))*y = 0. Defining ODE has Pfaffian-chain-2 coefficient (cos(2x)) on top of order-2 ODE => combined chain order 4. eml_cost.analyze reports pfaffian_r=4 for mathieuc and mathieus. Independent of Lerch (parametric oscillation, not Dirichlet series).
promotion_criteria: Add Mathieu-family rows to C-207-like dataset; verify pfaffian_r=4; literature check that Mathieu is not a sub-case of Lerch or any of the existing 8.
promotion_evidence: exploration/9th-tower-promotion-2026-04-27/: 25/25 hits (11/11 single-primitive + 14/14 composite) under chain-order additivity rule; 10/10 addendum rows in dataset_addendum.csv. Combined PNE corpus now 28/28 exact under the rule.
